question_structure.context: Given a transverse harmonic wave propagating along the $x$-axis, with $A{=}10\mathrm{cm}$ in both figures. Figure 1 shows the waveform at $t=2\mathbf{s}$, and Figure 2 shows the vibration image of the particle at the abscissa $x=2m$.
question_structure.sub_question_1: What is the direction of wave propagation and the wave speed?
question_structure.sub_question_2: What is the $y$ coordinate of the mass element at the horizontal coordinate $x=3m$ at time $t=15\mathrm{s}$?

sub_question_1.step_1: As can be seen from Figure 2, the mass element at $ x = 2 \mathrm{m} $ will vibrate in the -y direction after 2 seconds. Therefore, the wave propagates in the negative x-direction.
sub_question_1.step_2: As can be seen from the figure, $ T = 4 \mathrm{s} $.
sub_question_1.step_4: Then the wave speed is $ v = \frac{\lambda}{T} = 1 \mathrm{m/s} $.

sub_question_2.step_5: The waveform at \( t = 15 \, \text{s} \) is the same as the waveform at \( t = 3 \, \text{s} \), which is 1 second after the moment shown in Figure 1.
sub_question_2.step_6: Then it can be known that the y-coordinate of the mass element at $ x = 3 \mathrm{m} $ is 0.

answer: Propagating in the negative direction of the $x$-axis at $1\mathrm{m/s}$.
image_captions: The image shows two graphs, labeled Figure 1 and Figure 2. Both graphs have y-axes labeled "y" and x-axes. In both graphs the y-axis ranges from -A to +A. Figure 1's x-axis is labeled "x/m" and ranges from 0 to 6, indicating distance in meters. The graph in Figure 1 displays a sinusoidal wave with a wavelength of 4 meters. Figure 2's x-axis is labeled "t/s" and ranges from 0 to 6, indicating time in seconds. The graph in Figure 2 displays a sinusoidal wave with a period of 4 seconds. Both figures show a waveform oscillating about a horizontal line at y=0.

step_1.physical_theorem: Relationship between particle vibration direction and wave propagation direction

step_2.physical_theorem: Definition of period from vibration image

step_3.physical_theorem: Definition of wavelength from waveform diagram

step_4.physical_theorem: Relationship between wave speed, wavelength and period

step_6.physical_theorem: Relationship between wave motion and particle vibration

result_quantity.name: y-coordinate of the mass element at x=3m at t=15s
